title: aquilegia formosa subsp. pauciflora
intro: Aquilegia formosa subsp. pauciflora, also known as the few-flowered Himalayan columbine, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to help soothe wounds, reduce pain, and fight off infections. Research has shown that the plant's medicinal properties may also provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Aquilegia formosa subsp. pauciflora is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically prepared by steeping the plant's flowers and leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
